summary.en: Melbourne’s original bohemian suburb, its narrow, graffiti-adorned laneways are flanked by historic terrace houses and old pubs, buzzing with an eclectic, alternative energy.
With a grittier, post-industrial edge, this is where creative studios and galleries inhabit converted warehouses. It feels both historic and relentlessly modern.
The northern edge of the city grid combines Melbourne's civic and intellectual history with a treasure trove of hidden culinary gems, grand 19th-century buildings, and shops for the curious mind.
The quintessential Melbourne experience: a dense labyrinth of bluestone laneways and grand Victorian arcades that are now the city's pulse, home to street art, cafes, and restaurants.
Melbourne's cultural heart, defined by modern architecture, sprawling arts venues, and a riverside promenade offering some of the city's most iconic views.
A tale of two high streets, from Richmond's vibrant, down-to-earth pub culture to South Yarra's polished, upscale world of French bistros and designer boutiques.

description.en: A charming cafe and wine bar tucked away on a residential corner, perfect for a morning coffee or an afternoon wine.
A quintessential Melbourne corner pub that has been serving locals for generations, featuring a sun-drenched rooftop.
A world-renowned bakery in a sleek, warehouse-like space, famous for its technically perfect croissants and inventive pastries.
Wander Gore, George, or Napier Streets to admire well-preserved Victorian terrace houses with their intricate iron lacework.
An ever-changing outdoor gallery; duck into the alleys off Brunswick and Gertrude streets to find murals, stencils, and paste-ups.
The main artery of Fitzroy, buzzing with an eclectic, alternative energy and lined with cafes, pubs, and shops.
A key street for exploring Fitzroy's laneway art and home to many independent boutiques and creative spaces.
A bright, family-run Japanese cafe and concept store, perfect for a delicate breakfast and browsing curated homewares.
A unique venue that is both a community radio station and a bustling Italian restaurant with infectious energy.
A haven for independent Australian fashion and design. Take a slow walk and window-shop the thoughtfully curated stores.
Explore side streets off Wellington and Cambridge Streets to see stunning examples of industrial architecture and converted warehouses.
A heritage-listed public mural painted by the iconic artist during his visit in 1984, located on the wall of a former technical school.
A major thoroughfare in the neighborhood, home to key spots like Bar Liberty and the Keith Haring Mural.
A buzzing, no-frills Cantonese institution that's a local favorite for late-night meals, with legendary lobster noodles.
A key player in raising the profile of Filipino food in Melbourne, serving incredible and underrepresented dishes.
A genuine 'wonder-chamber' filled with scientific instruments, fossils, taxidermy, and other curiosities.
An architectural masterpiece and a center of Melbourne's intellectual history. The domed reading room is an iconic sight.
Australia's largest medical museum, featuring fascinating and sometimes macabre exhibits like a preserved 19th-century pharmacy.
The heart of Melbourne's Chinatown, home to legendary restaurants like Ling Nan and buzzing with energy.
A dimly lit, perpetually bustling wine bar with a phenomenal wine list and wood-fired cooking.
A stunning restaurant where cooking with fire is the main event, drawing influence from Chinese and Indonesian cuisines.
An opulent, sprawling restaurant that evokes the grand dining rooms of Chicago and London, perfect for a special occasion.
A hidden story on the top floor of the station: a magnificent, abandoned ballroom echoing with the ghosts of dances from a bygone era.
A classic Melbourne laneway, less crowded than Hosier Lane, offering a gritty and authentic cross-section of the city's street art scene.
A laneway dedicated to rock and roll, filled with street art and murals celebrating music legends.
A historic 19th-century shopping promenade with stunning mosaic floors, ornate plasterwork, and a beautiful glass ceiling.
Another of Melbourne's grand Victorian arcades, known for its elegant architecture and historic charm.
Melbourne's most famous street art laneway, a vibrant and constantly changing canvas of murals, stencils, and graffiti.
Located on the 55th floor of the Rialto, this bar offers incredible cocktails with a panoramic vista of the city.
Stand on Princes Bridge near Hamer Hall for the best ground-level photo of the skyline, framing the river and Flinders Street Station.
A uniquely Melbourne design of deconstructed, geometric shapes. Explore its hidden plazas and intricate facades.
A popular walking path along the river offering constantly changing perspectives of the city skyline.
A key venue in Melbourne's arts precinct and the landmark for finding the best ground-level city viewpoint.
A textbook French bistro and South Yarra institution that will make you feel like you've been transported to Paris.
A cult-favorite sandwich shop in Richmond that does not skimp on fillings, butter, or cheese. The HCT is a masterpiece.
A 'bánh mì bar' in Richmond specializing in khao jee pate, a delicious Lao-style breakfast sandwich.
A long commercial street that retains a gritty, energetic character, perfect for a casual stroll.
A green, peaceful walking and cycling path lining the river, offering a scenic break between Richmond and South Yarra.
Explore the 'Paris End' of this street to find high-end boutiques, art galleries, and opulent architecture.
An old-school bar on Brunswick Street, perfect for those who want to have a conversation over a drink.
A major cultural institution located in Carlton Gardens, known for hosting interesting adults-only nights.
A hidden gem of a bar located in the historic caretaker's cottage behind the Wesley Church on Lonsdale Street.
A popular Japanese-style cafe known for its unique food offerings and distinct atmosphere in the city.
A labyrinthine network of storm tunnels beneath the city, frequented by a dedicated community of urban explorers.
A sophisticated Middle Eastern restaurant tucked away on Bond Street, offering a unique dining experience in the CBD.
The city's former General Post Office, now a landmark retail hub with stunning heritage architecture on Bourke Street.
A popular bar hidden down Heffernan Lane, known for its great atmosphere and drinks.
A character-filled bar with a communist propaganda theme, tucked away on Liverpool Street.
A European-style restaurant and rooftop bar known for its unique decor and hidden cellar bar, Fall from Grace.
A classic, dimly-lit speakeasy hidden down Malthouse Lane, celebrated for its extensive cocktail list and theatrical presentation.
A themed cocktail bar in Corrs Lane, split into two distinct sections representing capitalist West and communist East Berlin.
A fairytale-themed rooftop cocktail bar above a bustling noodle house on Swanston Street.
A local favorite on Little Collins Street serving delicious Malaysian cuisine, especially praised for its rendang.
A small, non-touristy shop in Degraves Lane that serves authentic French-style baguettes and delicious waffles.

prompt.en: Find a laneway with vibrant graffiti and take a selfie that captures the alternative energy of Fitzroy.
Photograph the most intricate iron lacework you can find on a Victorian terrace house.
Frame a shot of a classic old pub on a street corner, capturing its historic character.
Take a photo of your coffee or snack from an independent cafe. How does it represent the neighborhood's creative soul?
Find a plaque, old sign, or architectural detail that hints at a building's past life.
Capture a photo that represents Fitzroy's unique and eclectic fashion sense (without being intrusive).
Find a piece of street art that isn't a mural—look for a small stencil, sticker, or paste-up.
Step off the main drag and capture a peaceful scene on a residential backstreet.
Photograph a dish or pastry from a local spot that looks like a work of art.
Find a historic building and try to imagine what it looked like 100 years ago. Capture it from a dramatic angle.
Create a photo collage of 4 different bold colors you find in Fitzroy's street art.
Photograph an interesting display in a quirky, independent shop window.
Find an example of nature reclaiming the urban environment, like a vine-covered wall or a plant growing in a crack.
Record a short video capturing the ambient sounds of Brunswick Street – trams, music, chatter.
Find a spot that feels like a local hangout and take a photo from that perspective.
Capture a photo that contrasts Collingwood's red-brick industrial heritage with a piece of modern street art or a contemporary building.
Find a converted warehouse and take a photo looking out from (or into) one of its large, industrial windows.
Take a selfie outside a creative studio, gallery, or independent boutique that captures the neighborhood's modern spirit.
Find evidence of local makers or artisans at work. This could be a sign for a studio, a unique handmade object, or a public art piece.
Take a close-up shot of an interesting texture you find in the neighborhood: peeling paint, old bricks, rusted metal, or worn concrete.
Find a sign, poster, or space that shows off Collingwood's strong sense of community.
Photograph an old factory building and try to capture a sense of its history and the industry that once thrived there.
Take a photo of your coffee or meal from a local cafe, presented in a way that reflects the area's design-focused aesthetic.
Find a modern building or extension and take a photo focusing on its clean lines and geometric shapes.
In this industrial zone, find a surprising pocket of green space—a community garden, a balcony overflowing with plants, or a leafy courtyard.
Photograph a piece of public art or a mural that you find on a main street, not hidden in a laneway.
Take a photo of the storefront of an independent boutique that you find most interesting.
Use the tall industrial buildings to your advantage and capture a dramatic photo of shadows on a wall or street.
Collingwood has a world-class food scene. Capture an overhead shot of your meal from a local eatery.
Find a building that has been converted from industrial to residential use and photograph a detail that shows both its past and present.
Find a grand 19th-century building and take a photo from a low angle to emphasize its scale and historic importance.
Take a selfie outside the State Library or another civic institution that makes you look thoughtful and scholarly.
Find the most curious or unusual object in a shop window and take a photo of it.
Photograph a meal or a drink from an establishment that feels like it has been around for decades.
Find a historical plaque or monument and photograph it in a way that connects it to the modern city around it.
Look for symmetry in the architecture of the grand old buildings and capture it in a perfectly balanced photo.
Find a quiet corner, perhaps near the library or in a park, and take a photo that captures a moment of peace and contemplation.
Capture the energy of a bustling market-adjacent street. Focus on the movement of people and goods.
Take a close-up photo of an ornate architectural detail on a historic building—a carving, a column, or a cornice.
This area has diverse food. Take a photo of a dish that represents a culture you're excited to try.
Photograph a flag, crest, or sign on a government or civic building that represents the city.
Find a shopfront or building that looks like it hasn't changed in 50 years and capture its vintage charm.
Stand at the base of a tall, historic building and take a photo looking straight up to the sky.
Take a photo that represents the pursuit of knowledge—it could be a bookstore, a university building, or people reading.
Capture a scene that feels like it could be from an old European city.
Take a photo down a narrow laneway that emphasizes its depth and mystery, making it look like a maze.
Get down low and capture a close-up of the iconic bluestone cobblestones, especially if they are wet and reflective.
Photograph the ornate ceiling or mosaic floor of one of the historic Victorian arcades.
Find the entrance to a hidden bar or a tiny hole-in-the-wall cafe and take a selfie as if you've just discovered a secret.
Find a piece of street art that tells a story and capture it. What do you think the artist is trying to say?
Capture the bustling energy of a crowded laneway. Use a slow shutter speed to create motion blur if you can.
Find a puddle or a shop window and capture a reflection of the laneway architecture or street art.
The laneways are full of smells from coffee roasters and kitchens. Take a photo of a place that smells amazing.
Find a spot where the light cuts dramatically through a narrow laneway, creating strong highlights and shadows.
Take a macro shot of a detail in a piece of street art—the texture of the spray paint, the edge of a stencil, or the layers of a paste-up.
Find a small table or stool at an outdoor cafe in a laneway and take a photo from that point of view.
Find a wall where you can see layers of old posters, graffiti, and paint, showing the history of the space.
Melbourne is famous for coffee. Capture a photo of your laneway coffee, focusing on the latte art or the rich color.
Find a neon sign in a laneway and capture its glow, especially as evening approaches.
Stand in the middle of a laneway and look up, capturing the slice of sky between the tall buildings.
From the river promenade or a bridge, capture a panoramic photo of the Melbourne CBD skyline.
Find the most interesting or unusual architectural angle within Federation Square's complex of buildings and photograph it.
Take a selfie in front of one of the major arts venues like Hamer Hall or the National Gallery of Victoria.
Capture the reflection of the city lights or buildings shimmering on the surface of the Yarra River.
Photograph the flow of people walking, jogging, or cycling along the Yarra River Promenade.
Take a close-up shot of the unique materials used in Federation Square's design—the sandstone, zinc, or glass facades.
Take a photo of your drink with the city skyline in the background from a riverside bar or restaurant.
Use one of the bridges over the Yarra as a frame for a photograph of the city or the river.
Find a piece of public sculpture or art in the area and take a creative photo with it.
Capture the warm light of sunrise or sunset hitting the glass buildings of the CBD from across the river.
Take a photo that represents the celebration of Indigenous culture in this precinct.
After dark, take a photo of the glowing city skyline and its reflection in the water.
Take a photo that includes the iconic Flinders Street Station as seen from the Southbank side.
Capture a photo of people relaxing and enjoying the public spaces along the river.
Photograph the interesting shadow patterns cast by the geometric shapes of Federation Square's buildings.
Take one photo that captures the gritty energy of Richmond's Bridge Road, and another that captures the polished elegance of South Yarra's Toorak Road.
Get a legendary sandwich from a Richmond deli and take the most epic, mouth-watering photo of it before you take a bite.
Find a scene in South Yarra that feels like it's been transported from Paris—a bistro, a boutique, or a stylish local.
Take a photo along the Yarra River Trail that captures the feeling of a peaceful, green escape from the city.
Photograph the exterior of a classic Richmond pub and a chic South Yarra bistro. What details define their different characters?
Find the most elegant or artistic window display in a South Yarra designer boutique.
Find a detail in Richmond that represents its down-to-earth, unpretentious character—a hand-painted sign, a local sports team scarf, or a classic milk bar.
Capture a photo of a pre-dinner drink, whether it's a beer at a Richmond pub or a glass of French wine in South Yarra.
Photograph a piece of opulent architecture in South Yarra and contrast it with the more functional, gritty buildings of Richmond.
Find a bicycle that perfectly sums up the vibe of the area it's in—is it a practical commuter in Richmond or a stylish cruiser in South Yarra?
Besides the river, find a small park, a leafy street, or a beautiful private garden that offers a moment of tranquility.
Find and photograph a sign with beautiful, classic French typography in South Yarra.
Take a photo from one of the bridges connecting the two neighborhoods, capturing the river and the greenery.
Take a selfie that shows which of the two neighborhood vibes—Richmond's casualness or South Yarra's polish—you connect with more.
Photograph a meal that perfectly represents its neighborhood: a hearty, no-fuss dish in Richmond or an elegant, classic plate in South Yarra.
Fitzroy is famous for its vintage and second-hand shops. Find the most unique item in a vintage store and snap a photo.
Capture the quintessential Fitzroy pub on a street corner. Bonus points for classic Victorian architecture.
Find a piece of street art that speaks to you and take a creative selfie with it.
Photograph an example of Fitzroy's unique and eclectic fashion sense, as seen on a local resident.
Discover a quiet backstreet or a hidden community garden and capture a moment of tranquility.
Take an artistic shot of your coffee or brunch from one of Fitzroy's many independent cafes.
Frame a shot that captures the contrast between a historic Victorian terrace house and a modern addition or piece of graffiti.
Find a wall covered in posters for local bands and live gigs. Capture the vibrant energy of the music scene.
Find the most unusual or funny business sign in the neighborhood and snap a picture.
Many of Fitzroy's best bars have unassuming entrances. Find one that looks like a secret and photograph it.
Find a bicycle that perfectly captures the Fitzroy vibe – maybe it's a vintage fixie or one covered in stickers.
Capture your reflection in the window of an interesting boutique on Gertrude or Brunswick Street.
Look closely for paper 'paste-up' art on walls and utility boxes. Get a detailed shot of your favourite one.
Record a 10-second video capturing the ambient sounds of Fitzroy – a tram bell, music from a bar, chatter from a cafe.
Find an intricate detail on a heritage building that most people would walk past without noticing. It could be iron lacework, a gargoyle, or a tiled entryway.
Capture the impressive facade of the State Library Victoria from an interesting angle.
Photograph the vibrant neon signs and lanterns of Chinatown on Little Bourke Street.
Take a selfie that captures the scholarly, student-filled atmosphere of the area near the universities.
Find a spot where you can photograph a historic building with a modern skyscraper looming behind it.
This area is a melting pot of cuisines. Photograph a dish from a Greek, Filipino, or other international eatery.
Seek out a hidden bar or cafe in a less-travelled laneway and capture its discreet entrance.
Stand in the middle of a quiet street and take a photo looking straight up at the towering buildings around you.
Find a bookshop and take a photo of its most interesting window display or a shelf of books.
Take a photo that captures the scale and modern architecture of the Melbourne Museum.
Capture the view down a long, straight city street like Lonsdale or La Trobe, showing the vanishing point.
Find a small park, square, or green space and capture a moment of calm amidst the city buzz.
Take a dynamic photo of a tram navigating the busy streets of the northern CBD.
Find a piece of public art or a sculpture and take a photo from a creative perspective.
Capture the diverse flow of people—students, shoppers, office workers—that defines this part of the city.
Find a unique, specialist shop (like Wunderkammer) and photograph its intriguing window display.
Get intentionally lost in a laneway and photograph the most unexpected thing you discover.
Capture the stunning architectural details of the Block Arcade or Royal Arcade, like the mosaic floors or glass canopy.
Find a bustling laneway cafe and take a close-up shot of a barista at work or your perfectly made coffee.
Take a selfie with your favorite piece of art in Hosier Lane or AC/DC Lane.
After it rains, find a puddle in a laneway and capture the reflection of the city lights and buildings.
Many of the best bars have unmarked doors. Find a mysterious-looking entrance and take a photo.
Stand in a narrow laneway and take a photo looking straight up at the sliver of sky between the buildings.
Photograph a delicious meal from a tiny hole-in-the-wall eatery that you can only find in a laneway.
Capture the constant motion and energy of the crowd under the iconic clocks at Flinders Street Station.
Get a low-angle shot that highlights the texture and pattern of the historic cobblestone laneways.
Find a rooftop bar and capture the view of the laneways and surrounding city from above.
Find a street performer or busker and capture a photo of them in action (ask for permission if you're close!).
Play with the dramatic light in the laneways. Capture a shot that emphasizes the contrast between bright light and deep shadows.
Take a close-up photo of the different textures you can find: peeling posters, brickwork, metal grates, and layers of graffiti.
Take a selfie with the historic Melbourne GPO building as your backdrop.
Capture the unique, deconstructed geometry of the buildings in Federation Square. Find the most interesting angle.
Stand on the Southbank promenade and take a selfie with the entire CBD skyline across the Yarra River behind you.
At dusk or night, photograph the colorful city lights reflecting on the surface of the Yarra River.
Frame a creative shot of the iconic Arts Centre Spire. Try shooting it from a low angle.
Capture the lively atmosphere of the Southbank promenade, showing the mix of locals, tourists, and street performers.
Take a photo from the middle of one of the pedestrian bridges, capturing the view down the river in either direction.
Find a piece of public art along the river and strike a pose that interacts with it.
Find a quiet spot on the grassy banks or steps by the river and take a photo that conveys a sense of peace.
Snap a photo of your drink at a riverside bar or restaurant, with the Yarra and the city in the background.
Get a shot of the unique Ponyfish Island bar, nestled under the pedestrian bridge.
Capture the sleek, modern architecture of the buildings along the Southbank promenade, like the Eureka Tower or Hamer Hall.
Use the iconic facade of Flinders Street Station as a backdrop for a photo taken from the south side of the river.
Photograph a tour boat, water taxi, or rowing crew making its way down the Yarra River.
Take a photo of the large public screen at Federation Square, capturing whatever is being broadcast.
Take a selfie on the steps or balcony of Hamer Hall, capturing its distinctive round shape.
